title: SSE buffer can grow unbounded when upstream never emits newlines
description:
## Severity
Critical
## Dimension
security
## Location
src/pipeline.rs:516-543
## Violation
The SSE parsing loop in `stream_sse_response` accumulates upstream bytes into an unbounded `String` until a `\n` is found. A hostile or broken upstream that streams large payloads without `\n` (or a single very long SSE event) will inflate `buffer` until the process OOMs. The only timeout (`last_data_time.elapsed()`) is reset by every byte received, so a 1 MiB/s newline-free stream is welcomed.

```rust
let mut buffer = String::new();
...
buffer.push_str(&String::from_utf8_lossy(&chunk));
while let Some(line_end) = buffer.find('\n') {
```
## Mandated refactor
Introduce `MAX_SSE_LINE_BYTES` and `MAX_SSE_BUFFER_BYTES` constants; abort the stream and emit `AppEvent::ApiError` when either is exceeded. Switch to `BytesMut` and `memchr` over raw bytes to avoid the per-chunk UTF-8 round-trip. Track total bytes against an absolute ceiling separate from the inactivity timeout.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
